agentPrompt: API error rate has gone up in the last 10 minutes for some users. Find the segment(s) where the regression is concentrated and what's going wrong.

expected.rootCauseHint: newly deployed enterprise-only cache-miss fallback path has a schema mismatch — only fires when tier=enterprise AND cache.hit=false

distractors.whyNotRootCause: Uniform 0.5% error rate across all segments and time — normal background noise, NOT the regression.

distractors.errorMessage: rec.compute timed out: upstream cf-feed unreachable
distractors.whyNotRootCause: Concurrent unrelated 502 burst on a different service. Tempting because it overlaps in time, but the prompt is about api-server (the customer-facing tier) and the affected traces have no recommendation-service span.

Enterprise tier has the same 0.5% baseline error rate as other tiers OUTSIDE the cache-miss path. Blaming enterprise on its own (without the cache axis) is incomplete — enterprise + cache.hit=true errors at baseline.
Cache misses on non-enterprise tiers error at the same 0.5% baseline. Blaming cache misses on their own (without the tier axis) is incomplete — pro/free + cache.hit=false errors at baseline.

programmatic: (root cause|caused by|primary issue|the issue is|the cause is)[^.\n]{0,80}?\b(global|api-server (alone|generally)|all (tiers|users|traffic)|service[- ]wide)
(root cause|caused by|primary issue|the issue is|the cause is)[^.\n]{0,80}?\bcache[ \-]?(miss(es)?|lookup|hit\s*=\s*false)(?![^.\n]{0,200}?(enterprise|tier))
(root cause|caused by|primary issue|the issue is|the cause is)[^.\n]{0,80}?\brecommendation-service

criteria.description: Did the answer correctly identify the affected segment as the INTERSECTION of `tenant.tier=enterprise` AND `cache.hit=false` on `api-server`? Identifying only ONE axis (just the tier OR just the cache state) is a correctness failure — the bug only fires on the intersection, and quoting an aggregate over a single axis (which dilutes the signal) misses the actual problem.

Did the answer (a) note the rough magnitude (e.g. ~12% error rate in the affected segment vs ~0.5% baseline), (b) reference the specific error signature (schema mismatch / enterprise_v2 / FallbackHandlerError or the fallback handler path), and (c) explain why neither single-axis aggregation reveals the issue?

Did the answer rule out the distractors? Specifically: (a) the concurrent recommendation-service incident is on a different service and unrelated. (b) Enterprise-tier alone is NOT the cause — enterprise + cache.hit=true errors at the baseline rate. (c) Cache misses alone are NOT the cause — pro/free + cache.hit=false error at baseline. (d) The global api-server error rate is dominated by baseline noise; the regression is invisible without segmenting. Mistaking any of these for the root cause is a calibration failure.
Was the answer concise, well-structured, and free of filler or unnecessary speculation?
